perturbation.original_text: 4. Voting Commitments. The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date (if the 2020 Annual Meeting is within thirty (30) days of the anniversary of the Company's 2019 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, such number of shares shall be no less than 90% of the Current Position) for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the right to vote the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties in their sole discretion with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting. The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Voting Commitments. The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote 50% of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the right to vote the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties in their sole discretion with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting. The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by specifying that the White Hat Parties only need to vote 50% of their shares in accordance with the Board's recommendations, whereas the first sentence of section 4 states that the white hat parties must vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date. This directly conflicts with the initial requirement to vote all shares, leading to ambiguity regarding the extent of their voting obligation. This impacts the enforceability of the agreement, particularly Section 7(a), which allows for specific enforcement of strict adherence to the agreement. The **in-text contradiction** makes it unclear whether specific enforcement should apply to all or only half of the shares.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Voting Commitments. The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote 50% of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the right to vote the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties in their sole discretion with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting. The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states the White Hat Parties only need to vote 50% of their shares in accordance with the Board's recommendations. However, the unchanged first sentence of section 4 initially required them to vote all of the shares. This directly conflicts, creating an in-text contradiction and ambiguity. The definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction matches this scenario, indicating that key obligations are defined inconsistently, hindering enforcement and creating interpretive uncertainty. The altered version directly contradicts the initial requirement, making the contradiction obvious and impactful.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Support Period Covenants. Except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, at all times during the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the date that is thirty (30) days prior to expiration of the advance notice period for the submission by stockholders of director nominations for consideration at the 2021 Annual Meeting as set forth in the Bylaws, the White Hat Parties shall not, and shall cause the White Hat Representatives not to, directly or indirectly, in any manner, alone or in concert with others: (a) attempt to call a special meeting of stockholders of the Company; or (b) make a request for any stockholder list or other Company books and records, whether pursuant to Section 220 of the Delaware General Corporation Law or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Support Period Covenants. Except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, at all times during the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the date that is thirty (30) days prior to expiration of the advance notice period for the submission by stockholders of director nominations for consideration at the 2021 Annual Meeting as set forth in the Bylaws, the White Hat Parties may, and shall cause the White Hat Representatives to, directly or indirectly, in any manner, alone or in concert with others: (a) attempt to call a special meeting of stockholders of the Company; or (b) make a request for any stockholder list or other Company books and records, whether pursuant to Section 220 of the Delaware General Corporation Law or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an **in-text contradiction** within Section 5 by stating that the White Hat Parties 'may' attempt to call a special meeting of stockholders and 'may' request stockholder lists, whereas the original text explicitly stated they 'shall not' do these things. This creates uncertainty regarding their obligations during the support period. It impacts the interpretation of Section 5 as a whole, making it ambiguous whether the White Hat Parties are restricted or permitted to engage in these activities. This particularly contradicts the initial clause of Section 5, which states 'Except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement,' implying restrictions, not permissions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Support Period Covenants. Except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, at all times during the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the date that is thirty (30) days prior to expiration of the advance notice period for the submission by stockholders of director nominations for consideration at the 2021 Annual Meeting as set forth in the Bylaws, the White Hat Parties may, and shall cause the White Hat Representatives to, directly or indirectly, in any manner, alone or in concert with others: (a) attempt to call a special meeting of stockholders of the Company; or (b) make a request for any stockholder list or other Company books and records, whether pursuant to Section 220 of the Delaware General Corporation Law or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent and wording of Section 5. The original text states the White Hat Parties 'shall not' perform certain actions, while the modified text says they 'may' perform those same actions. This creates a direct in-text contradiction as defined by Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, because it makes their obligations uncertain and opens the door to conflicting interpretations. It falls under the definition of ambiguity because the core obligations of the White Hat Parties during the support period become unclear due to conflicting language, rendering Section 5 internally inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. Additionally, there is contradiction by structural flaw where in the same location of a paragraph it is making conflicting statements.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Miscellaneous. (a) Specific Enforcement; Special Remedy. Each of the Parties agrees that the other Party would be irreparably injured in the event that any provision of the Agreement is breached or not performed. Accordingly, it is agreed that each Party shall be entitled to temporary and permanent injunctive relief with respect to each and any breach or purported repudiation of this Agreement by the other and to specifically enforce strict adherence to this Agreement and the terms and provisions hereof against the other in any action instituted in a court of competent jurisdiction, in addition to any other remedy which such aggrieved Party may be entitled to obtain. Moreover, in the event of the breach of any of the provisions of this Agreement, timeliness in obtaining relief is of the essence.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Miscellaneous. (a) Specific Enforcement; Special Remedy. Each of the Parties agrees that the other Party would be irreparably injured in the event that any *material* provision of the Agreement is breached or not performed. Accordingly, it is agreed that each Party shall be entitled to temporary and permanent injunctive relief with respect to each and any breach or purported repudiation of this Agreement by the other and to specifically enforce strict adherence to this Agreement and the terms and provisions hereof against the other in any action instituted in a court of competent jurisdiction, in addition to any other remedy which such aggrieved Party may be entitled to obtain. Moreover, in the event of the breach of any of the provisions of this Agreement, timeliness in obtaining relief is of the essence.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an **in-text contradiction** within Section 7(a) regarding the standard for specific enforcement. By adding the word 'material' before 'provision,' it implies that only breaches of *material* provisions warrant injunctive relief and specific enforcement, however the remaining parts of the Section 7(a) enforce strict adherence to this Agreement and the terms and provisions hereof. This change undermines the original intent of Section 7(a) which states that specifically enforce strict adherence to this Agreement and the terms and provisions hereof. This ambiguity could lead to disputes over whether a breach qualifies for specific enforcement, particularly in light of the original text. The change directly impacts how courts will interpret and enforce the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Miscellaneous. (a) Specific Enforcement; Special Remedy. Each of the Parties agrees that the other Party would be irreparably injured in the event that any *material* provision of the Agreement is breached or not performed. Accordingly, it is agreed that each Party shall be entitled to temporary and permanent injunctive relief with respect to each and any breach or purported repudiation of this Agreement by the other and to specifically enforce strict adherence to this Agreement and the terms and provisions hereof against the other in any action instituted in a court of competent jurisdiction, in addition to any other remedy which such aggrieved Party may be entitled to obtain. Moreover, in the event of the breach of any of the provisions of this Agreement, timeliness in obtaining relief is of the essence.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'material' before 'provision' in Section 7(a) creates a direct contradiction within the same section. Initially, 'each Party shall be entitled to temporary and permanent injunctive relief with respect to each and any breach,' implying all provisions are subject to specific enforcement. By adding 'material,' it suggests only breaches of material provisions warrant such relief, contradicting the latter part of the section which enforces 'strict adherence to this Agreement and the terms and provisions hereof.' This creates an ambiguity regarding which breaches qualify for specific enforcement, directly undermining the original intent and creating a strong in-text contradiction.
